search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk CG184 dyspepsia follow-up symptoms alarm endoscopy H pylori review referral
site:dha.gov.ae dyspepsia guideline primary care UAE
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic functional dyspepsia, reflux, ulcer, or malignancy diagnosis
automatic PPI, H. pylori test, endoscopy, medicine, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Current diagnosis and response require clinician review.
No treatment, investigation, or action is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with established or suspected dyspepsia follow-up; children, pregnancy, bleeding, dysphagia, weight loss, severe pain, postoperative, and malignancy contexts require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary-care documentation and follow-up as qualified by the exact source and clinical context.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE primary-care, laboratory, prescribing, referral, safeguarding, and local-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult dyspepsia section supports interval epigastric pain, heartburn, regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, meal context, precipitants, and change without confirming dyspepsia cause or response.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports adult recurrent epigastric pain, heartburn, acid regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, associated precipitants, meal timing, and clinician-discussed lifestyle documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact alarm and endoscopy section supports clinician-assessed bleeding, medicine cause, new alarm features, available examination, and prior endoscopy context without asserting absence or ordering a procedure.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports immediate specialist referral for significant acute gastrointestinal bleeding, medicine-cause review, prior endoscopy review, and further assessment when new alarm signs are present.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact investigation-review section supports recording already reviewed H. pylori and prior investigation context without interpreting results.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed H. pylori testing context and annual review for people needing long-term dyspepsia management; medicine names and doses are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact management and referral section supports clinician-entered discussion, medicine-review status, safety net, follow-up, and referral status without generating treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented reflux management, medication-plan discussion, review, and specialist referral where symptoms do not respond or remain unexplained; no dose or autonomous treatment text is mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Diagnostic basis, symptom values, alarm features, medicine, adherence, response, H. pylori, endoscopy, diagnosis, referral, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
